data.title: Helmerich & Payne says nine more rigs suspended in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Helmerich & Payne (NYSE:HP) -0.9% in early trading Thursday after falling 4% in the previous session following disclosure that it received notices of contract suspensions for an additional nine rigs in the legacy KCA Deutag rig fleet operating in Saudi Arabia, bringing the company's total rig suspensions in country to 26 rigs.

"Although the unexpected softening in KCA Deutag acquisition's Saudi operations has presented short-term financial challenges, H&P continues to maintain its strong financial foundation," President and CEO John Lindsay said. "Even with the slowdown in the Saudi market, we are beginning to capture synergies from this transaction and remain confident that our significant drilling presence in the world's most productive oil and gas region enhances the company's long-term position in the global market."

Lindsay also said Helmerich & Payne's (NYSE:HP) North America Solutions operations and other markets are performing in-line with previously stated guidance.

data.title: Citizens Financial rolls out new suite of Mastercard credit cards
data.description:
Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG), in collaboration with payment network titan Mastercard (NYSE:MA), has launched a new suite of credit cards built on customers' insights and feedback, it said on Thursday.

The cards are designed to meet the evolving needs of every customer, offering tailored features that range from establishing credit to maximizing rewards and lifestyle benefits. 

“This launch with Mastercard is about more than just offering new options; it’s about delivering real tools to help customers build credit, manage spending, and unlock new opportunities," said Adam Boyd, Head of Lending at Citizens.

New credit card options include: Citizens Amp Mastercard, Citizens Spring World Mastercard, Citizens Summit World Mastercard and Citizens Summit Reserve World Elite Mastercard.

What's more, Citizens (NYSE:CFG) unveiled a new card designed specifically for Private Bank clients.

The lender is also developing new commercial card solutions tailored to the evolving needs of middle market companies, it said, with a customizable product for growth-stage businesses expected to roll out later this year.

data.title: Airbus forecasts demand for 43,400 new aircraft in next 20 Years
data.description:
The global aviation sector is poised for decades of growth that will outlast short-term uncertainties, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) said Wednesday in its latest market outlook.

The aircraft manufacturer projects a 3.6% annual increase in passenger traffic through 2044, driven by long-term trends including rising global GDP and expanding urban populations. A major source of demand will be a growing middle class, which expected to swell globally by 1.5 billion people, most of whom are statistically more likely to travel by air.

To meet this rising demand, 43,400 new passenger and freighter aircraft will be needed globally in the next 20 years. Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) estimates that 34,250 of those will be single-aisle jets, while 9,170 will be widebody aircraft. Nearly 44% of these deliveries (18,930 aircraft) will replace older, less fuel-efficient models.

While mature markets remain important, Airbus highlights a surge in growth across emerging regions. India leads all domestic markets with projected traffic growth of 8.9% annually, followed closely by emerging Asia to China (8.5%) and the Middle East to emerging Asia (5.3%).

data.title: Medtronic separate diabetes business to be called MiniMed
data.description:
• Medtronic's (NYSE:MDT) stand-alone diabetes company, first announced in May, will be known as MiniMed.
  • MiniMed was the original name of the diabetes company Medtronic acquired in 2001.
  • Medtronic said it is aiming to complete the separation by November 2026.

data.title: Airline stocks are on watch following the Air India crash, rising Middle East tension
data.description:
The airline sector was jolted early on Thursday by the crash of a London-bound Air India plane right after takeoff from Ahmedabad, India. While it is too early to determine why the Air India plane crashed, the incident marks the first time a Boeing (BA) 787 has crashed.

Notably, the 787 Dreamliner fleet was grounded in 2013 after fires related to its lithium-ion batteries, which were part of the aircraft’s electrical power system. Today, the 787 is used by several airlines, including All Nippon Airways, United Airlines (UAL), American Airlines (AAL), Qatar Airways, Japan Airlines (OTCPK:JAPSY) (OTCPK:JPNRF), Etihad Airways, British Airways (OTCPK:ICAGY), Air Canada (OTCQX:ACDVF) (AC:CA), Hainan Airlines, and Air India. 

Traders are also closely watching the developments in the Middle East, amid reports that Israel is considering taking military action against Iran without U.S. support in the coming days. A major conflict in the Middle East could lead to sharply higher jet fuel prices.

Yesterday, airfares in the U.S. were reported to have fallen 7.3% year-over-year in May, in another sign that demand could be cracking in certain categories. Airfares in the U.S. have now fallen for four straight months, which has a direct impact on revenue and profitability in the airline sector.

U.S. airline stock check: American Airlines (AAL) -2.5%, Delta Air Lines (DAL) -2.2%, Southwest Airlines (LUV) -1.8%, United Airlines (UAL) -2.9%, JetBlue Airways (JBLU) -2.4%, Alaska Air Group (ALK) -1.6%, Allegiant Travel (ALGT) -2.7%, Spirit Airlines (OTC:SAVEQ), Mesa Airlines (MESA) -1.1%, SkyWest (SKYW) -2.0%, Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) -2.8%, and Frontier Group (ULCC) -3.3%.

European airline stocks are broadly lower in late Thursday trading.

data.title: A.I. Joe: Mattel recruits OpenAI to design toys -- update
data.description:
Updated 9:40am ET: Deal between Mattel and OpenAI was confirmed here. 

Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) will start using artificial intelligence to design the next generation of toys and even movies through a deal with OpenAI.

“Leveraging this incredible technology is going to allow us to really reimagine the future of play,” Mattel’s (NASDAQ:MAT) chief franchise officer, Josh Silverman said in a statement.

By employing AI tools, Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) can create new toys based on its existing brands, personal assistants based on its current stable of characters, and even make existing toys more interactive. ChatGPT will also be incorporated into Mattel's (NASDAQ:MAT) business operations to enhance product development and innovation. 

The company will continue to retain all intellectual property and remains in full control of the products it creates through AI, Silverman added. The first release won’t be announced until later this year.

Mattel's (NASDAQ:MAT) partnership with OpenAI could accelerate its shift toward creating movies, TV shows, and interactive games based on iconic brands like Barbie, GI Joe, and Barney. The recent launch of Mattel Studios underscores this strategic pivot, aiming to bring its franchises to both the big and small screens.

For OpenAI, the deal with Mattel (MAT) introduces a new avenue for the adoption of its artificial intelligence tools. The company has been courting Hollywood for its new AI video generator, demonstrating the capabilities of its Sora model which can generate short video clips based on user prompts, as well as extend existing short videos.

But Mattel (MAT) is not the first to incorporate AI into its creative process. Smaller toy manufacturers are already employing AI tools to assist in concept art, as well as the workflow of production. The technology has already been incorporated into logistics, as well as voice-recognition for popular toys like Barbie and in the design of more visually appealing packaging.

data.title: Crypto giant Binance launches in Syria after U.S. sanctions lift
data.description:
Crypto exchange Binance Holdings said Thursday it is expanding operations to Syria, enabling local users to access its platform for the first time as U.S. sanctions are rolling back.

In mid-May, U.S. President Donald Trump said he will order an end to the sanctions on Syria to give the country's new government "a chance of peace."

"Now that sanctions have been suspended, the Syrian community can finally act on that [crypto] interest," Binance said in a statement.

Now, Syrian residents can use Binance to access its crypto-focused products and services, from spot and futures trading to cross border remittances.

As recently as 2021, Syria ranked among the top 10 countries globally crypto crypto-related search activity, the company noted.

data.title: Stocks open lower as geopolitical, trade tensions weigh; bonds rally on soft PPI
data.description:
U.S. stocks opened lower on Thursday, as sentiment was weighed down by geopolitical risks and trade tensions. However, bonds rallied on a benign producer inflation report.    

Minutes after the bell, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.3%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.6%.

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported a +0.1% M/M rise in the producer price index (PPI) for May vs. +0.2% expected and -0.2% in April. The core PPI also rose +0.1% vs. +0.3% consensus and -0.1% in April. 

U.S. Treasury yields moved sharply lower after the PPI report, as traders snapped up bonds. The 10-year yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.37%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 3.90%.

Despite the soft PPI data, headlines on Thursday were dominated by rising geopolitical risks in the Middle East, a renewed tariff threat by U.S. President Donald Trump, and a crash of an Air India Boeing (BA) aircraft.    

Trump said he intended to send letters to trading partners in the next week or two setting unilateral tariff rates, ahead of a July 9 deadline to reimpose higher duties on dozens of countries.

"Trump said they would send letters to U.S. trading partners, detailing the threatened 9 July unilateral trade taxes. Market reactions have been a muted negative; investors have become conditioned to expect retreats from such threats. However, the uncertainty created may weigh on US investment and hiring in the near term," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Traders also continued to await any details on the U.S.-China trade deal. 

On geopolitical news, the U.S. government on Wednesday ordered the departure of embassy staff from Baghdad after Iran threatened to attack U.S. bases if talks over its nuclear program fall through.

"Sentiment wasn’t helped by various headlines, and CBS News reported that part of the reason for the U.S. moving staff out of the Middle East was because U.S. officials had been told that Israel was ready to launch an operation into Iran," Reid said.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

data.title: Conagra faces meaningful headwinds from protein inflation, demand elasticities - BofA Securities
data.description:
Facing cost pressures for chicken, beef and pork exacerbated by limited pricing power, Conagra (NYSE:CAG) faces a “meaningful” headwind to earnings growth along with increased margin pressures, leading BofA Securities to downgrade the packaged food producer to Underperform from Neutral, and shave 26% off its price target.

“Conagra faces unique challenges within packaged food heading into FY26,” said BofA’s Peter Galbo, as the company’s largest category – single-serve frozen meals— already has elevated demand elasticities.

The company recently debuted more than 50 new frozen foods options, including gluten-free and plant-based meals, and sold its Chef Boyardee, Mrs. Pauls’ and Van de Kamp’s brands. Although necessary, Galbo believes these actions will also likely be a drag on earnings over the next year, especially in consideration of decelerating sales in its frozen foods category and its exposure to elasticities from protein-cost relating pricing.

For the current fiscal year, the company estimated COGS inflation at ~4% which is offset by an underlying ~4% COGS productivity. But in FY26, Galbo estimates an unchanged COGS productivity of ~4% to be more than offset by ~6% COGS inflation. And given Conagra’s (NYSE:CAG) limited pricing price, FY26 gross margin could compress by as much as 100-150 basis points from FY25.

“Our Underperform rating is predicated on our view that earnings will be meaningfully pressured by reacceleration of inflation for COGS basket in FY26 without significant pricing power…in addition CAG’s FY26 leverage targets and dividend payout ratio appears to be at risk,” Galbo says in Thursday’s note to investors.

Galbo’s current $20 price target suggests 10% downside to Wednesday’s closing price.

Conagra (NYSE:CAG) shares opened 2% lower, setting a new 6 ½ year low.
